skill.name: Azure.ResourceManager.MySql (.NET)
skill.description: Azure Resource Manager SDK skill for managing MySQL Flexible Server deployments in .NET. Provides code examples for creating servers, databases, firewall rules, configurations, backups, and high availability setup.
skill.summary: Azure Resource Manager SDK for managing MySQL Flexible Server deployments in .NET
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill that provides code examples and reference documentation for the Azure.ResourceManager.MySql SDK. No executable code, scripts, or network operations are present in the skill itself. The skill contains only markdown documentation and example code snippets for SDK usage. Static analysis confirms zero security issues with a risk score of 0/100.

content.value_statement: This skill provides ready-to-use code examples for Azure Resource Manager MySQL Flexible Server operations in .NET. Developers can quickly implement database provisioning, firewall configuration, backups, and high availability without searching through documentation.
actual_capabilities: Create and configure MySQL Flexible Server instances with customizable SKUs, storage, and HA options
Manage databases including creation, charset, and collation settings
Configure firewall rules for IP access control and Azure service access
Update server configurations such as max_connections, buffer pool size, and query logging
Set up Entra ID (Azure AD) administrator authentication
Perform backup operations and point-in-time restore
Stop, start, restart, and scale MySQL server instances
limitations: This skill provides code examples only - actual execution requires proper Azure subscription and credentials
Does not include runtime database query operations - use MySqlConnector or similar for data access
Requires Azure subscription with appropriate permissions for resource management
Does not cover MySQL Single Server (deprecated) migration scenarios

use_cases.title: DevOps Engineer automating infrastructure
use_cases.description: Automate MySQL database provisioning and configuration as part of infrastructure-as-code pipelines using .NET and Azure SDK
use_cases.target_user: DevOps engineers building CI/CD pipelines for Azure deployments

use_cases.title: Backend Developer managing databases
use_cases.description: Quickly implement database management operations in .NET applications without studying full SDK documentation
use_cases.target_user: Backend developers working with Azure and .NET

use_cases.title: Solutions Architect designing architectures
use_cases.description: Reference proven code patterns for MySQL Flexible Server deployment with HA, backup, and security configurations
use_cases.target_user: Solutions architects designing Azure database architectures

prompt_templates.title: Create a MySQL Flexible Server
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a MySQL Flexible Server in Azure using the Azure.ResourceManager.MySql SDK with the following requirements: East US location, GeneralPurpose SKU, 128GB storage with auto-grow enabled, 7-day backup retention, and zone-redundant high availability
prompt_templates.scenario: Provisioning a new production MySQL database with recommended settings

prompt_templates.title: Configure firewall rules
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure firewall rules for an Azure MySQL Flexible Server using the .NET SDK. I need to allow access from a specific IP range (10.0.0.1 to 10.0.0.255) and enable access for all Azure services
prompt_templates.scenario: Setting up network access controls for database security

prompt_templates.title: Set up Entra ID authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: How do I configure Entra ID (Azure AD) administrator for MySQL Flexible Server using the .NET SDK? I want to use managed identity for authentication
prompt_templates.scenario: Enabling Azure AD authentication for improved security

prompt_templates.title: Backup and restore operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to list available backups for a MySQL Flexible Server and perform a point-in-time restore to create a new server instance

output_examples.input: Create a MySQL Flexible Server with zone-redundant HA
output_examples.output: Code example showing MySqlFlexibleServerData creation with HighAvailability configured for ZoneRedundant mode, including SKU, storage, backup settings, and availability zone specification
Code example demonstrating how to get and update MySQL server configuration parameters like max_connections, innodb_buffer_pool_size, and slow_query_log using the Configuration collection
Code example showing MySqlFlexibleServerPatch usage to update SKU and storage properties for scaling up or down

best_practices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication instead of connection strings to leverage managed identity and Entra ID
Enable zone-redundant high availability for production workloads to ensure business continuity
Configure Entra ID authentication instead of SQL authentication for improved security
anti_patterns: Hardcoding administrator passwords in code - use Azure Key Vault or managed identities instead
Using MySQL Single Server (deprecated) instead of Flexible Server for new deployments
Disabling SSL/TLS for database connections - always use SslMode=Required

faq.question: What is the difference between MySQL Flexible Server and Single Server?
faq.answer: Flexible Server is the modern deployment option with auto-scaling, better pricing, and more features. Single Server is deprecated and scheduled for retirement.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure MySQL using .NET?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which supports managed identity, service principal, and interactive authentication. For database access, you can also use Entra ID token-based authentication.

faq.question: Can I use this skill to run SQL queries?
faq.answer: No, this skill is for Azure Resource Manager operations (infrastructure management). For running queries, use MySqlConnector or similar data access libraries.

faq.question: What SKUs are available for MySQL Flexible Server?
faq.answer: Three tiers: Burstable (B1ms, B2s) for dev/test, GeneralPurpose (D-series) for production, and MemoryOptimized (E-series) for high-memory workloads.

faq.question: How do I implement high availability?
faq.answer: Configure HighAvailability with Mode set to ZoneRedundant or SameZone. ZoneRedundant provides HA across availability zones for disaster recovery.

faq.question: How do I reduce costs for development environments?
faq.answer: Use Burstable SKU for dev/test, configure auto-stop schedules, and use the StopAsync method to pause servers when not in use.
